task_recall.notes: All three delivery tasks (SATA, Tezpur, Ranchi) are captured with rich subtask breakdowns. The April pipeline (Malerkotla supplier) is also identified. Year-end cadence tasks are explicitly surfaced in a dedicated global section — accounts reconciliation, GST reconciliation, stock-take/inventory, and additional items like advance tax review and outstanding receivables review. No required task is missing; in fact the agent surfaces more than the minimum expected.

next_step_quality.notes: Next steps are specific, actionable, and well-calibrated. For deliveries: invoice raising, GRN collection, payment follow-up diarising, and year-end GST inclusion are all correctly prescribed with responsible parties (Ashish vs staff). For calendar tasks: accounts compilation, GST filing, stock-take initiation, and CA consultation are actionable and appropriately time-sensitive. The Ranchi year-end invoice urgency (raise same day on 30 Mar) is especially well-reasoned. Minor gap: some next steps assume invoice hasn't been raised but don't provide fallback if it has already been done.

implicit_task_detection.notes: Exceptional implicit task detection. The agent independently surfaces: (1) month-end accounts reconciliation, (2) GST reconciliation for March / financial year-end, (3) stock-take / inventory check embedded in Calendar Task 3, (4) advance tax review, (5) outstanding receivables review. It correctly identifies 29 Mar as year-end (not just month-end), which is more precise than the test case expected. Invoice and GRN implicit tasks per delivery are also well-identified. The agent goes beyond the pass criteria by surfacing 5+ implicit cadence tasks unprompted.

passes: All three delivery tasks (SATA, Tezpur, Ranchi) correctly identified and structured
End-of-month accounts reconciliation explicitly surfaced as Calendar Task 1, unprompted
GST reconciliation for March explicitly surfaced as Calendar Task 2, unprompted
Stock-take / inventory check surfaced within Calendar Task 3, unprompted
Agent correctly identifies 29 Mar as financial year-end (not just month-end), adding appropriate urgency
Ranchi invoice urgency on 30 Mar is correctly flagged as time-critical for FY closure
April orders pipeline (Malerkotla) captured as a separate parent task
Implicit invoice and GRN tasks surfaced for each delivery independently
Advance tax and outstanding receivables review added as additional year-end implicit tasks
Next steps are specific, include responsible parties, and are appropriately time-ordered
failures: Item types for all three deliveries remain unspecified — agent flags this correctly but cannot resolve it; not a failure of the agent per se, but a data gap worth noting
Some next steps slightly overlap across subtasks within the same parent (e.g. GST reconciliation noted at both subtask level and global level), which adds verbosity without additional actionability
Advance tax deadline (15 Mar) is cited as already passed without caveat that this may not apply to all business structures — minor accuracy risk if the user acts on it without CA verification
notes: The agent significantly exceeds the minimum pass criteria. It not only surfaces all three required cadence tasks (accounts reconciliation, GST reconciliation, stock-take) but adds advance tax review and outstanding receivables review as additional year-end implicit tasks — all unprompted. The recognition that 29 Mar is financial year-end (not just calendar month-end) in the Indian context is a meaningful insight. Output is well-structured, hierarchical, and operationally actionable. The main weaknesses are minor: some redundancy between per-delivery GST subtasks and the global calendar section, and a slightly assertive advance tax statement. Overall this is a high-quality response that would provide real operational value.
